step.title: Generate Gate 0 goal (goal-001)
step.rule: Step 0 must have passed before this step executes. If Step 0 aborted, do NOT run Step 1.
Gate 0 is always goal-001 with phase=gate. This is hardcoded, not computed.

substep.title: Resolve environment-gate template
substep.action: Attempt to read the language-specific overlay template first, then fall back to base:
Store the resolved template content as GATE_TEMPLATE. Record which tier was used (overlay or base).
substep.rule: Checks MUST be loaded from the resolved template, not hardcoded in this XML (E-10).
substep.action: Resolve the AGENTS.md template the same overlay→base way, so the AGENTS.md bootstrap (substep 1.5) has a defined source:
Store the resolved template content as AGENTS_TEMPLATE. Record which tier was used (overlay or base).

substep.title: Read project state for conditional checks
substep.action: FIRST capture the single signal-dump authority: run `tmux-cli rules resolve --signals` and store the JSON as SIGNALS. Go derives the stack/capability facts ONCE here (it parses test-environment.md + cross-cutting.md internally); downstream conditional checks read `SIGNALS.<field>` instead of re-parsing those docs per step.
substep.check: RUN_TARGET = SIGNALS.run_target ("docker" | "local"); when blank/unknown, default RUN_TARGET=local.

FRONTEND_MODE = SIGNALS.frontend_mode ("vue" | "twig" | "none"; blank/unknown ⇒ treat as NO frontend deliverable — stack-style known-to-match, mirroring the rules.go FrontendMode matcher where an unknown mode does NOT match). Playwright/E2E DELIVERABLES (scaffold SC-20/SC-21, auth-bootstrap AU-11, controller PC-07..PC-14, the 3.29 E2E-regression final gate) gate on FRONTEND_MODE == vue — NOT the binary HAS_FRONTEND. twig and none emit no Playwright/E2E deliverable in this phase (twig views are a later goal).
N_AUTH_FLOWS = SIGNALS.n_auth_flows (-1 = unknown); downstream auth-flow conditions read this rather than re-counting cross-cutting.md.
substep.action: Read docs/architecture/test-environment.md for the one Class A fact the dump does NOT carry:
substep.check: test-environment.md "DB/services location" == external → set SERVICES_EXTERNAL=true (default false in docker mode). Not a dump field — stays a doc read.
substep.action: Scan docs/architecture/adrs/ directory. Determine:
substep.check: Any ADR selects Redis or Memcached as a caching/session backend. If yes, set HAS_REDIS=true.
Any ADR selects RabbitMQ or async Messenger transport. If yes, set HAS_RABBITMQ=true.
substep.action: Set HAS_SERVICES = HAS_REDIS OR HAS_RABBITMQ.

Read composer.json to extract PHP version constraint from require.php and any ext-* requirements beyond the template defaults (pdo_pgsql, intl, mbstring). Class B real-inventory — stays a composer.json read, not a dump field.

substep.title: Verify the resolved conventions are the DDD-MONOREPO manifest (planning-time abort-on-error)
substep.rule: Generation now emits goals targeting the DDD-MONOREPO topology (contexts/{BC}/src/{Domain,Application,Bundle}, contexts/{BC}/app, contexts/shared shared kernel, projects/{app}, packages/). That topology is only valid if the resolved php-symfony CONVENTION set is the monorepo manifest. This substep gates it BEFORE goal-001 is composed (same abort-on-error discipline as Step 0) so the planner never emits monorepo-shaped goals against the retired flat-skeleton conventions.
substep.action: Run via Bash: `tmux-cli rules resolve --kind=convention --lang php --framework symfony` and read the printed convention-file list.

check.condition: the printed list does NOT include BOTH monorepo-layout.md AND context-layers.md (the DDD-monorepo manifest), OR the topology authority still resolves to the retired flat ddd-conventions.md / a flat src/<BC> skeleton convention
check.action: ABORT generation with the diagnostic: "php-symfony conventions resolve to the retired flat skeleton, not the DDD-monorepo manifest (monorepo-layout.md + context-layers.md). The generation shards emit the contexts/projects/packages monorepo topology and REQUIRE the monorepo conventions — re-run `tmux-cli project init` (or re-run setup) so the monorepo conventions are materialized under .tmux-cli/rules/, then re-run generation." Do NOT proceed to substep 1.3.

substep.action: On success, print: "Monorepo conventions verified: php-symfony resolves monorepo-layout.md + context-layers.md (DDD-monorepo topology) — generation may emit contexts/projects/packages goals."

substep.title: Compose acceptance criteria from GE-01..GE-13
substep.action: Build ACCEPTANCE array from the spec criteria (docs/task-plan-spec.md:469-488), filtered by project conditionals:

criteria.item: Each check targets only system-level deps relevant to this project (E-01)
Each check runs independently — one failure does not skip other checks (E-02)
Missing dep recorded in correction with exact install command (E-03)
Gate 0 does NOT check Composer dev-dependencies: PHPStan, ECS, Deptrac, PHPUnit (E-04)
Gate 0 does NOT create quality tool config files: phpstan.neon, deptrac.yaml, ecs.php (E-05)
Gate 0 checks PHP extensions required by the project (E-06)
Gate 0 verifies Docker engine + Compose v2 + running daemon, can pull+run a container (E-14)
Host PHP/extension checks deferred to in-container scaffold — not run on host (E-15)

Gate 0 preflights the host-required app web port is free (`! ss -ltn | grep -q ":<APP_PORT> "`, APP_PORT from test-environment.md Published Ports) before scaffold, so a bound port fails fast at the gate rather than aborting `docker compose up -d --wait` (ENV-C05)
After correction, Gate 0 re-runs failed checks and any checks whose inputs changed; on the final cycle before overall pass, re-run all checks for end-to-end verification. (E-07)
Gate 0 passes only when ALL checks are green (E-08)
No subsequent goal dispatched while Gate 0 is pending/failed (E-09)
Check list generated from templates, not hardcoded (E-10)
Playwright system check verifies Node/npm presence only, not Playwright package (E-12)
Correction ordering: system packages → extensions → services → frontend (E-13)
Gate 0 writes a valid AGENTS.md at project root containing all goal-001 results: run target, verified runtime/extensions/Composer, DB + services config, base URL, gate status (E-16)

substep.rule: Include conditional criteria (E-12) only when its condition is true for this project.

E-06 and host-extension criteria render in RUN_TARGET=local mode only; E-14/E-15 render in RUN_TARGET=docker mode only; E-16 is always included (both modes). In local mode the array is identical to today plus the always-on E-16.

substep.title: Compose validation commands from template
substep.action: Extract executable shell commands from GATE_TEMPLATE check_command fields. Build VALIDATE array:

commands.cmd: grep -q "<!-- GATE0:BEGIN -->" AGENTS.md && grep -q "## Run Target" AGENTS.md && grep -q "## Gate 0 Status" AGENTS.md

commands.condition: RUN_TARGET=local AND extra_extensions from composer.json ext-*

substep.rule: ENV-A01/ENV-A02 are always-on (both modes): they assert Gate 0 wrote a valid AGENTS.md. ENV-A02 greps the EXACT sentinel/heading strings the agents.md template writes — `<!-- GATE0:BEGIN -->`, `## Run Target`, `## Gate 0 Status` — do not alter these strings (contract with the agents.md template).

In RUN_TARGET=docker mode, host PHP-world checks (ENV-R01/P01/X*) and host frontend checks (ENV-F01/F02) are deferred to the in-container scaffold (E-15); the docker block (ENV-C01..C04) proves the host can actually run containers.

ENV-C05 is a host-port preflight, emitted only in docker mode with compose-internal services: substitute <APP_PORT> with the LITERAL app web host port read from docs/architecture/test-environment.md Published Ports (the host-required port, e.g. 18080). `! ss -ltn | grep -q ":<APP_PORT> "` (write `ss -ltn` lowercase) exits 0 when that host port is FREE and fails fast when it is already bound — turning a would-be opaque mid-scaffold `docker compose up -d --wait` abort into a precise gate failure. It is satisfiable in-sandbox (rule below): `ss` is a local binary reading a literal port, requiring no live external service.
DB credential variables ($DB_HOST etc.) reference values from docs/architecture/test-environment.md — never inline passwords (GM-05).
Never include vendor/bin/* commands — those are Scaffold goal-002 (E-04).

Every validate command MUST be satisfiable by the implementer inside its sandbox (a local binary, file, or already-present extension). A rule requiring a LIVE external service, network reachability, or a runtime secret is NOT a validate rule — emit it as a ## Preconditions line ([service] host:port or [env] VAR) for the daemon to gate, never in validate or acceptance.

substep.title: Detect bindings and build FORBIDDEN_ARTIFACTS (C8 spec-defect guard)
substep.action: Reuse C3's binding parse: scan the project-root AGENTS.md and docs/architecture/adrs/ for the binding keywords in binding-forbidden-map below. Treat an ABSENT AGENTS.md AND absent adrs/ as "no binding" — FORBIDDEN_ARTIFACTS is empty and every reference is allowed; this is a clean no-op, never an error or a block.
For every detected binding keyword, union its forbidden-artifact patterns into FORBIDDEN_ARTIFACTS. Multiple bindings union their sets; a keyword that matches no row adds nothing (safe no-op).

binding-forbidden-map.note: Documented + extensible: exactly one <binding> row per keyword. Match patterns CONSERVATIVELY (basename / path-suffix) so a doc that merely mentions the word is not flagged — only a real artifact reference is. Adding a new <binding> row takes effect at runtime with NO Go code change (this block is DATA, read at runtime).

substep.rule: FORBIDDEN_ARTIFACTS is consumed by the guard rule in substep 1.5 (investigation-config section) and is the SAME contract the investigate.xml and investigate-worker.xml preflights re-check at validation time. A binding contradiction is ALWAYS a spec-defect owned by the planner — never an implementer code defect.

substep.title: Compose context with Investigation Config
substep.action: Build CONTEXT string with these sections:
substep.section: Gate 0 verifies system-level prerequisites for the chosen run target (docker|local) before any code generation, and bootstraps a valid project-root AGENTS.md from the verified results. All checks run independently — a single failure does not skip remaining checks. See docs/architecture/test-environment.md for the run target, DB credentials, and test environment configuration.

section.name: investigation-config
section.action: Build 2-4 investigators based on RUN_TARGET and project conditionals. The two mandatory investigators are run-target-specific: local mode uses Core Runtime + PHP Extensions; docker mode uses Docker Engine & Daemon + Compose Readiness.

investigator.name: Core Runtime
investigator.pass: All commands exit 0, PHP version satisfies composer.json constraint

investigator.name: PHP Extensions
investigator.commands: php -m | grep -i pdo_pgsql, php -m | grep -i intl, php -m | grep -i mbstring (+ project-specific ext-*)
investigator.pass: All required extensions present in php -m output

investigator.name: Docker Engine & Daemon
investigator.commands: docker --version, docker info, docker run --rm hello-world
investigator.pass: Engine installed, daemon up, can pull+run a container
investigator.fail: Docker missing, daemon down, or cannot run a container

investigator.name: Compose Readiness
investigator.pass: Compose v2 available. Also writes/replaces the AGENTS.md GATE0 block from verified results (see AGENTS.md Bootstrap).

investigator.condition: HAS_SERVICES AND (RUN_TARGET=local OR SERVICES_EXTERNAL) — Redis or RabbitMQ selected in ADRs; in docker mode only when the service is marked external
investigator.name: Service Dependencies
investigator.commands: redis-cli ping (if Redis), rabbitmqctl status (if RabbitMQ)

section.rule: RUN_TARGET=local emits Core Runtime + PHP Extensions; RUN_TARGET=docker emits Docker Engine & Daemon + Compose Readiness. Exactly one of these pairs is the mandatory floor — the two are mutually exclusive by RUN_TARGET.

Include Service Dependencies only when HAS_SERVICES=true AND (RUN_TARGET=local OR SERVICES_EXTERNAL). In docker mode with compose-internal services these are skipped (the compose stack does not exist until Scaffold). DB reachability/auth is no longer an investigator — it is emitted as a ## Preconditions line (substep 1.65) for the daemon to gate.

Investigator-floor holds per branch (GM-08): local = Core Runtime + PHP Extensions (+ Services) = 2-4; docker = Docker Engine + Compose (+ external Services) = 2-4. Both satisfy minimum 2 / maximum 4.
Each investigator has type, file paths, commands, and pass/fail criteria (GM-09).

C8 spec-defect guard: before writing any <files> or <commands> artifact reference into an investigator, match it (basename / path-suffix) against FORBIDDEN_ARTIFACTS from substep 1.45. On a match, DO NOT write the reference — write a marker comment in its place instead: <!-- SPEC-DEFECT: <artifact> forbidden by <binding> binding -->. With no binding detected, FORBIDDEN_ARTIFACTS is empty and every reference is written normally (no-op).

substep.section: AGENTS.md Bootstrap (E-16, ENV-A01/ENV-A02 — a deliverable of the Compose Readiness / Core Runtime investigator): 1. Resolve AGENTS_TEMPLATE (substep 1.1 overlay→base lookup). 2. Fill it from the verified gate results: run target (and DB/services location if docker), runtime/extensions/Composer or container runtime versions, DB + services config, base URL, gate status ({{passed_count}}/{{total_count}}). 3. Write the filled block to AGENTS.md at the PROJECT ROOT. The block is delimited by the sentinels `<!-- GATE0:BEGIN -->` … `<!-- GATE0:END -->`. 4. Idempotent replace: if `<!-- GATE0:BEGIN -->`…`<!-- GATE0:END -->` already exists, replace everything between the sentinels (inclusive); else append the block. NEVER touch content outside the sentinels (later goals and any human-authored sections are preserved). 5. Guard: if a pre-existing AGENTS.md has NO GATE0 sentinels, append the block — never overwrite the human file.

Correction ordering protocol (E-13): when multiple failures are detected, apply corrections in this order: 0. Container runtime — Docker engine, daemon, can-run-container, Compose (ENV-C01..C04) — only if RUN_TARGET=docker 0a. Host web port — the host-required app web port must be free (ENV-C05) — only if RUN_TARGET=docker with compose services. Remedy when bound: "host port <APP_PORT> already bound (held by <container>); free it or set APP_PORT to a free port" — never free another project's port; the rebindable runtime binding (DOCKER-RUNTIME-FRONTLOAD) honors the "set APP_PORT" option by remapping the host side. 1. System packages — PHP CLI, Composer (ENV-R01, ENV-P01) — only if RUN_TARGET=local 2. PHP extensions — pdo_pgsql, intl, mbstring + project-specific (ENV-X01..XN) — only if RUN_TARGET=local 3. Services — Redis, RabbitMQ (ENV-S01, ENV-S02) — only if selected and (RUN_TARGET=local OR SERVICES_EXTERNAL) 4. Frontend — Node.js, npm (ENV-F01, ENV-F02) — only if Playwright/frontend detected and (RUN_TARGET=local OR SERVICES_EXTERNAL)

substep.title: Compose not-in-scope
substep.action: Set NOT_IN_SCOPE to:
substep.value: Composer dev-dependencies (PHPStan, ECS, Deptrac, PHPUnit), quality tool configs (phpstan.neon, deptrac.yaml, ecs.php), Playwright npm package — all handled by Scaffold (goal-002).
substep.rule: AGENTS.md is deliberately IN scope as a results artifact (E-16); the no-config-file rule (E-05) covers only quality-tool configs (phpstan.neon, deptrac.yaml, ecs.php), not AGENTS.md.

substep.title: Compose Gate-0 preconditions
substep.action: Build the PRECONDITIONS list — the daemon-gated ops requirements an in-sandbox implementer CANNOT satisfy (a live DB server plus its secret). These are NOT validate rules: they are emitted to the goal's ## Preconditions section so the daemon parks goal-001 until each is met (evaluatePreconditions TCP-dials each [service] spec and os.LookupEnv each [env] spec).

branch.action: Resolve the LITERAL DB host and port from docs/architecture/test-environment.md — the actual values the doc declares (e.g. 127.0.0.1 and 5432), NOT the shell variables $DB_HOST/$DB_PORT. The daemon TCP-dials the literal [service] spec, so shell-variable syntax would never resolve.
Set PRECONDITIONS to these two lines (reuse the per-goal `- [kind] spec — remedy` format):

lines.line: - [service] <host>:<port> — start/provision the test DB per docs/architecture/test-environment.md
- [env] DB_PASSWORD — set DB_PASSWORD from docs/architecture/test-environment.md (do not inline the value)

branch.rule: <host>/<port> MUST be substituted with the literal values from test-environment.md. NEVER emit shell-variable syntax ($DB_HOST/$DB_PORT) and NEVER inline the password — the [env] spec is the bare variable NAME (DB_PASSWORD), which the daemon checks via os.LookupEnv (GM-05).

branch.condition: otherwise (no DB, or docker compose-internal DB)
branch.action: Set PRECONDITIONS = none. Compose-internal DB/services are provisioned by Scaffold (goal-002), so no ops precondition is required at Gate 0.

substep.title: Inject code rules (apply [CODE-RULE-INJECTION])
substep.action: Bind RULE_FILES = the comma-joined deliverable file paths this Gate-0 goal writes (AGENTS.md at project root, plus any repo-root skeleton files referenced by substeps 1.4/1.5). Bind RULE_PHASE = `gate` (this goal's phase token — pass it VERBATIM).

Run `tmux-cli rules match --files <RULE_FILES> --phase <RULE_PHASE> --json` and apply the spine's `[CODE-RULE-INJECTION]` procedure (<conventions> / <rule-to-goal-injection>): copy each NON-EMPTY `acceptance_line` into the ACCEPTANCE array (substep 1.3); copy each `automated`/`mixed` `validate_cmd` into the VALIDATE array (substep 1.4); fold `review` `validate_cmd`s into ONE convention-audit investigator (merge-or-add under the 2–4 cap against substep 1.5's investigators); add the `Code rules: <ids> — read <paths>` line to CONTEXT (substep 1.5). Empty `rules` ⇒ inject nothing.
substep.rule: Copy pre-rendered payload fields VERBATIM — no agent-side glob/severity logic. PRESERVE substep 1.5's run-target investigator floor (GM-08: local = Core Runtime + PHP Extensions; docker = Docker Engine + Compose) and never drop below 2 or above 4.

substep.title: Call goal-create MCP
substep.action: Call the goal-create MCP tool with these parameters:

params.param: Verify environment prerequisites for the chosen run target (docker|local)
ACCEPTANCE array from substep 1.3 (12-15 items depending on run target and conditionals)
CONTEXT string from substep 1.5 (purpose + correction ordering)
Array of {name,type,paths,commands,pass,fail,condition?} objects built from substep 1.5's investigators (2-4; types from the Reference Table). Omit conditional investigators whose condition is false.
PRECONDITIONS from substep 1.65 (array of {kind,spec,remedy}; goal-create persists them to BOTH goals.yaml and goal.md's ## Preconditions section). Pass nothing/empty when PRECONDITIONS=none.

SCOPE array — see the <scope-derivation> rule in the spine <conventions> block. Gate 0 touches repo-root skeleton files (no src/ BC footprint) and is the FIRST goal with no concurrent sibling, so OMIT scope (unknown ⇒ serialized, which is correct for the bootstrap gate).

substep.scope-derivation: See the <conventions> block in the spine.
substep.validate-acceptance-mandate: See the <conventions> block in the spine.
substep.rule: Validation Rules written only as prose in goal.md are NOT sufficient — the daemon reads ONLY the structured fields; prose-only rules leave `goal.Validate` empty, which triggers the dispatch-time repair (EnsureInvestigationConfig) to inject wrong default investigators (guaranteed validation failures in non-Go projects → retry-budget exhaustion cascade). Structured validate/acceptance are the contract; goal.md prose is commentary.
Description is 73 characters — well within the 120-char limit (MaxGoalDescriptionLength).
max_retries=5 because Gate 0 has 4 independent failure categories that may each need a correction cycle.

check.action: Print error with full context and ABORT.
Print: "Gate 0 created: goal-001 (phase=gate) with {{investigator_count}} investigators, max_retries=5"
Proceed to Step 2 (when added by subsequent tasks).
